title: Fix refund eligibility logic for Poland non-delivery cases
description: Alfred denies refunds for non-delivery claims in Poland because it applies global policy that requires photo evidence. Polish market rule allows automatic refund for non-delivery within 30 minutes if courier GPS confirms no arrival.
evidence: 38 escalations in PL refund category this week (↑62% WoW). Agent override rate 42%. CSAT 3.1.
expected_impact: Reduce PL escalation rate from 27.8% to ~16%. CSAT improvement to 3.8+.
label: Logic

title: Add disambiguation for wrong charge vs. promo code classification
description: Alfred misclassifies duplicate charge and wrong fare complaints as promo code issues when both involve payment amounts. Creates immediate wrong routing and requires agent override.
evidence: Affects India (28 cases/wk), Nigeria (19 cases/wk), South Africa (12 cases/wk). Override rate 38% in wrong charge category.
expected_impact: Reduce wrong charge escalation rate from 23.8% to ~14%. Fix classification for ~190 weekly conversations.
label: Classification

title: Create Nigeria-specific payout delay knowledge article
description: Alfred uses global payout timelines (1-3 biz days) for Nigeria. Actual timeline is 4-7 business days due to partner bank constraints. Drivers get false expectations and re-contact causing escalations.
evidence: NG payout escalations up 34% WoW. 47 driver contacts per week. Repeat contact rate 62%.
expected_impact: Reduce NG payout escalation rate from 34% to ~18%. Reduce repeat contact by ~40%.
label: Knowledge Gap

title: Investigate and fix Germany vehicle telemetry API instability
description: Germany scooter telemetry API showing 12-14% error rate this week (↑8% from baseline). Alfred has no timeout fallback, exposing customers to 15-22 second waits then generic errors.
evidence: DE vehicle unlock failure rate 14.2% this week. 89 affected conversations. Tool error rate 12.4% in DE.
expected_impact: Restore DE vehicle unlock automation rate from 54% to ~72%. Reduce tool error rate to <2%.
label: Tooling

title: Build empathy-first template for safety complaint responses in Mexico
description: Safety complaints in Mexico receive technically correct but emotionally cold responses. Customers rating CSAT 1-2 even when reports are accepted. Response lacks acknowledgment of fear, urgency, and cultural sensitivity.
evidence: MX safety CSAT 3.1 vs. global 3.8. 28 safety complaints this week, avg CSAT 2.4. Agent rewrites response tone in 61% of cases.
expected_impact: Improve MX safety CSAT from 3.1 to 3.8+. Reduce agent rewrite rate from 61% to <15%.
label: UX/Tone

title: Add ghost trip detection signal to payment dispute logic
description: Alfred trusts driver GPS 'arrived' event without verifying rider proximity. Ghost trips (driver marks arrival without pickup) classified as completed trips, blocking legitimate refunds.
evidence: 12 confirmed ghost trip complaints in NG this month. 100% escalation rate. High fraud signal pattern.
expected_impact: Catch ~80% of ghost trip fraud before human review. Save ~$3,200/month in fraudulent charges.
label: Logic

title: Add 8-second timeout + fallback for vehicle telemetry API calls
description: Currently no timeout is set on vehicle API calls. Alfred waits indefinitely, causing 15-22s dead responses. Quick fix: add 8s timeout and trigger fallback response while engineering fixes root cause.
evidence: Same issue as BLG-004. This is the interim fix. Engineering root cause fix is BLG-004.
expected_impact: Reduce customer wait time from 18s avg to <8s. Improve experience even when API fails.
label: Tooling

title: Update Kenya food delay refund trigger (40+ min delay = auto refund offer)
description: Alfred deflects explicit refund requests for delayed food with 'please wait more'. Customers with 40+ min delays should receive proactive refund offer, not a waiting response.
evidence: 18 cases/week in KE food delay category. Reopen rate 28%. CSAT 3.2.
expected_impact: Reduce KE food delay reopen rate from 28% to <10%. Improve CSAT by 0.4 points.
label: Logic

title: Expand Germany account verification automation (KYC API stabilized)
description: Account verification automation in Germany is at 44% due to previous KYC API instability. API is now stable. Opportunity to expand automation coverage with proper fallback in place.
evidence: DE account verification volume: 240/wk. Current automation 44%. API error rate now <1% (down from 8%).
expected_impact: Increase DE account verification automation from 44% to 68%. Deflect ~58 cases/week.
label: Expansion Opportunity

title: Add P0 safety incident routing — immediate human handoff with empathy SLA
description: Safety incidents involving physical fear, assault, or threat should never be fully handled by Alfred. Need P0 routing that detects severity and immediately hands off to trained safety agents with <2 min SLA.
evidence: MX safety incident CSAT 1-2. CONV-8581 example of cold response to assault-adjacent complaint. Compliance and Trust & Safety flagged this as critical risk.
expected_impact: Eliminate AI-only safety complaint handling for P0 cases. Compliance risk mitigated.
label: Compliance

title: Localize Alfred response phrasing for Nigerian English register
description: Alfred responses in Nigeria use formal British English that feels distant to Nigerian users. Tone calibration for Nigerian market would improve trust and reduce perceived coldness.
evidence: NG CSAT 3.3 vs global 3.8. Sentiment analysis shows 'cold', 'robotic', 'unfriendly' keywords in 18% of NG feedback.
expected_impact: Improve NG CSAT by 0.3-0.5 points. Reduce negative tone sentiment flags by ~40%.
label: Localization

title: Expand Estonia promo code automation — CSAT 4.2, low reopen
description: Estonia promo code automation performing well (CSAT 4.2, reopen 6.1%). Volume has grown 22% in last 4 weeks. Can safely expand coverage and add 3 new promo types.
evidence: EE promo CSAT 4.2, automation 78%. Zero compliance flags. Agent override rate 3.1%.
label: Expansion Opportunity

title: Confidence calibration audit — cross-tab confidence vs reopen by market/intent
description: Alfred's confidence scores are not calibrated at market/intent granularity. A global confidence threshold of 0.75 is applied uniformly, but this threshold is meaningless in markets/categories where Alfred's accuracy at 0.80+ is still 60% wrong. Need per-cohort calibration curves.
evidence: Poland Refund cohort: 0.81 avg confidence, 18.2% reopen rate. MX Safety P0: 0.77 confidence on cases that required immediate human escalation. Global confidence looks fine; local calibration is broken.
expected_impact: Reduce overconfident wrong resolutions by ~40%. Better threshold tuning per market/intent will prevent dangerous high-confidence failures.
label: Classification

title: Handoff quality scoring — measure CSAT and handle time on escalated cases
description: Currently we measure escalation rate but not escalation quality. When Alfred escalates, does the human agent receive a useful context package? We have no visibility into handle time on escalated cases, CSAT on escalated cases vs Alfred-resolved cases, or how often agents need to ask customers to repeat themselves.
evidence: CONV-8581 (MX safety escalation): Alfred handoff summary read 'safety report filed.' Agent received a customer in distress expecting a billing agent. Handle time: 24 minutes. CSAT: 1.
expected_impact: Identify top 20% of escalations with poor handoff. Fix context package quality. Reduce escalated case handle time by ~30%.
label: Logic

title: Define and enforce knowledge freshness SLA — 14-day product-to-Alfred update window
description: There is no SLA between 'product team ships a change' and 'Alfred knows about it.' Right now the gap is discovered reactively — when customers start getting wrong answers. Need a formal process: product change → knowledge impact assessment → Alfred knowledge update within 14 days.
evidence: Kenya food delivery insurance policy launched 3 weeks ago. Alfred still says 'no such policy' — 100% override rate on these queries. NG payout policy update delayed 47 days. Every delay creates a systematic failure window.
expected_impact: Reduce knowledge staleness incidents by 80%. Every product change should trigger an Alfred knowledge review as standard practice.
label: Knowledge Gap

title: Agent override categorization — distinguish tone vs policy vs escalation overrides
description: All agent overrides are currently logged as a single metric (override rate 9.1%). But tone overrides, policy overrides, and escalation overrides require completely different fixes. A tone override means Conversation Design problem. A policy override means CS Product or Local Ops problem. An escalation override means Logic or Compliance problem. Mixing them gives no actionable signal.
evidence: Agent interviews suggest ~35% of overrides are 'I just softened the wording' vs ~40% 'I changed the actual decision.' These are completely different failure modes but tracked as the same number.
expected_impact: Transform override rate from a single vanity metric into 3 actionable signals routing to the right owner teams. Estimated 30% reduction in override rate within 2 sprints of categorized fixes.
label: Knowledge Gap
